title: FW Idea: Enable ability to schedule repeater adverts so they can flood during low airtime periods

messages.author: Nathan AI7NC
messages.text: Airtime utilization from 1am-4am seems to be almost an order of magnitude lower than the peak airtime usage during the day. If we had the ability to schedule repeater auto-adverts sometime during the late night, it would free up a lot of daytime utilization for messages. Adverts represent roughly 20% of all packets heard by monitors in PDX region. Nodes heard based on adverts are 81% repeaters. With feature like this and participation from repeater owners we could shift roughly 16% of our network utilization to a time period where there's much less impact to users and the network as a whole. This could be as simple as a hardcoded block in time in which a time slot is selected randomly to avoid many repeaters all trying at once.
messages.content_html: Airtime utilization from 1am-4am seems to be almost an order of magnitude lower than the peak airtime usage during the day. If we had the ability to schedule repeater auto-adverts sometime during the late night, it would free up a lot of daytime utilization for messages. Adverts represent roughly 20% of all packets heard by monitors in PDX region. Nodes heard based on adverts are 81% repeaters. With feature like this and participation from repeater owners we could shift roughly 16% of our network utilization to a time period where there's much less impact to users and the network as a whole. This could be as simple as a hardcoded block in time in which a time slot is selected randomly to avoid many repeaters all trying at once.
messages.reactions_html: 3

messages.author: mxda
messages.text: Would love this, get all the adverts pushed out in the early morning one by one as if it's an overnight update
messages.content_html: Would love this, get all the adverts pushed out in the early morning one by one as if it's an overnight update

messages.author: Nathan AI7NC
messages.text: I've started a feature request to open the discussion: https://github.com/meshcore-dev/MeshCore/issues/1949
